title: Replace regex HTML converter with html-to-markdown-rs in web_fetch
task: Replace the home-grown regex-based HTML-to-markdown and HTML-to-text converters in web_fetch with the html-to-markdown-rs crate (version 3.5.4). The current converter (lines 265-419 of fetch.rs) uses static regexes to strip tags and convert simple HTML structures, but it misses tables, nested elements, and produces garbled output on complex pages. html-to-markdown-rs is a proper HTML parser (html5ever-based) with preprocessing presets that strip boilerplate (nav, forms, sidebars). The tool's external interface (schema, return format) must not change.

requirements.description: Add html-to-markdown-rs = "3.5.4" as a direct dependency in crates/norn/Cargo.toml. No feature flags needed beyond the default (metadata).
requirements.acceptance: html-to-markdown-rs = "3.5.4" appears in [dependencies] of crates/norn/Cargo.toml and cargo check --workspace passes

requirements.description: Replace the html_to_markdown() function (lines 352-409 of fetch.rs) with a call to html_to_markdown_rs::convert(). Configure ConversionOptions with: preprocessing preset Aggressive, remove_navigation true, remove_forms true, compact_tables true. Return the content field from ConversionResult. If conversion fails, fall back to returning the raw HTML body (do not error the tool call).
requirements.acceptance: html_to_markdown() calls html_to_markdown_rs::convert() with Aggressive preprocessing, remove_navigation, remove_forms, compact_tables. Conversion error returns raw body. No regex-based markdown conversion remains.

requirements.description: Replace the html_to_text() function (lines 325-350 of fetch.rs) with a call to html_to_markdown_rs::convert() using OutputFormat::Plain and the same Aggressive preprocessing options. If conversion fails, fall back to returning the raw HTML body.
requirements.acceptance: html_to_text() calls html_to_markdown_rs::convert() with OutputFormat::Plain. Conversion error returns raw body. No regex-based text conversion remains.

requirements.description: Remove the entire regexes module (lines 265-323 of fetch.rs) and the decode_entities function (lines 411-419). These are dead code after R2 and R3.
requirements.acceptance: No regexes module, no decode_entities function, no static OnceLock regex compilation remains in fetch.rs. No regex import needed for HTML conversion.

requirements.description: The convert() dispatch function (lines 251-263), the Format enum, the tool schema, the tool return shape (url, format, bytes, truncated, content_type, content), and the WebFetchTool struct must remain unchanged. This is a pure internal swap — external behavior stays the same.
requirements.acceptance: Tool schema is identical. Return JSON shape is identical. Format enum has same three variants. Existing tests that check tool output structure still pass.

context: File: crates/norn/src/tools/web/fetch.rs (601 lines total including tests). The regex converter is lines 265-419. The convert() dispatch function at line 251 calls html_to_markdown() or html_to_text() based on the format parameter. The WebFetchTool struct at line 51 owns a reqwest::Client. html-to-markdown-rs is already in Cargo.lock at v3.4.1 as a transitive dep via kreuzberg, so adding it directly just pins the version. The crate's API is: html_to_markdown_rs::convert(html, Some(options)) -> Result<ConversionResult>. ConversionResult.content is Option<String>. ConversionOptions is built via ConversionOptions::builder().preprocessing(PreprocessingOptions { enabled: true, preset: PreprocessingPreset::Aggressive, remove_navigation: true, remove_forms: true }).compact_tables(true).build().
